% Supplementary Table S27: Metric Definition Audit
\begin{tabular}{llllll}
\toprule
Definition group & Quantity & Operational definition & Computation or scope & Interpretation boundary & Source artifact or code \\
\midrule
Input normalization & x\_i expression vector & Processed nonnegative platform matrix with standardized gene symbols and aligned unit metadata; task features apply the registered task-layer transforms rather than pooling raw CosMx and Xenium scales directly. & LR/module and marker summaries use log1p or unit-interval summaries where registered; label centroid features use log1p plus row L2 normalization; CellPack rows use rank/order and expression-bin tokens from the same matrix. & Cross-platform summaries aggregate route/utility metrics after task-level normalization; they are not claims that raw platform count depths are interchangeable. & manuscript Section 3.3; src/cellpack/evaluation/*; src/cellpack/sequence\_builder.py \\
Utility and regret & u\_im and r\_im & u\_im is the registered route-label or benchmark-matrix metric utility mapped to [0,1]; r\_im equals the in-setting empirical oracle utility minus u\_im. & Route-level utilities come from the registered route utility matrix summarized in Supplementary Table S34; CP-Q6 metric rows use the normalized/proxy metric\_value fields generated by the benchmark matrix. & The oracle is the best registered method in the evaluated set, not an absolute biological or algorithmic performance ceiling; CP-Q2 route regret measures label alignment. & Supplementary Table S34; src/cellpack/method\_comparison.py; results/reports/cp\_q6\_benchmark\_matrix/benchmark\_metric\_matrix.csv \\
Effect-control evidence gate & a\_i\_eff and source q-values & Binary registered effect-control flag used in Equation 5. For split-level rows, the source artifacts may include BH-adjusted q-values for their own context-vs-reference contrasts; for synthetic rows, the flag is the registered control-consistency gate. & ContextGate reads the source flag as fdr\_effect\_gate\_passed. A numeric q-value, when present, belongs to the source comparison table and is not reinterpreted as a one-sided test of the aggregate Delta\_i sign gate. & The effect-control flag can pass when the residual-utility sign gate fails because the two gates are different registered evidence checks; context\_allowed requires both. Within-split BH screens are nominal and do not control the aggregate 40/63 pass count or spatially dependent tests globally. & src/cellpack/evaluation/router\_target\_mining.py; results/reports/cp\_q6\_benchmark\_matrix/benchmark\_metric\_matrix.csv \\
Safety metrics & all-row false-context exposure & Fraction of all evaluated rows, regardless of null status, where a method uses context on a row labeled null, wrong-context, or unsupported. & Uses all rows as the denominator, unlike Equation 3 FCR, whose denominator is restricted to n\_i = 1 rows. & This is a population-level exposure descriptor and need not be the complement of denominator-aware no-harm unless both are computed on the same row set. & Supplementary Table S24; results/reports/cp\_q6\_benchmark\_matrix/benchmark\_metric\_matrix.csv \\
Safety metrics & FCR, no-harm, specificity & False-context rate is context use among null or unsupported rows; no-harm is one minus that rate; null specificity is non-context routing among synthetic null/control ladder rows. & Pooled and denominator-stratified calculations are reported separately so wrong-context, unsupported real-data, and synthetic-null rows are not silently conflated. & Expression-only and ContextGate can share FCR=0; ContextGate's added value is safety plus positive-control detection and auditable abstention. & Supplementary Table S24; Supplementary Table S35; results/reports/cp\_q2\_method\_comparison/method\_predictions.csv; results/reports/cp\_q3\_positive\_null\_control\_ladder/positive\_control\_ladder.csv \\
Positive/null ladder & sensitivity score and specificity score & Continuous diagnostic scores stored in the CP-Q3 sensitivity and specificity columns and summarized as row means with normal-approximation mean intervals. & The positive-control sensitivity score is averaged over the 36 target-observable positive rows; the null/control specificity score is averaged over the 36 registered negative/control rows and repeated without panel-blocked rows in Supplementary Table S35. & These score means are distinct from the binary route recovery/rejection proportions in Equation 4; for ContextGate, 0.8633 and 0.9433 are score means, while 36/36 and 36/36 are binary route outcomes. & Supplementary Tables S19 and S35; results/reports/cp\_q3\_positive\_null\_control\_ladder/positive\_control\_ladder.csv; results/reports/cp\_q3\_positive\_null\_control\_ladder/method\_summary.csv \\
Positive/null ladder & gate\_pass\_rate & Mean of the per-row gate\_passed indicator in the synthetic positive/null ladder for a method family. & Positive rows pass when sensitivity clears the registered floor and route is context-positive; negative rows pass when specificity clears the floor, no false context is used, and route is negative or abstained. & This is control-consistency over synthetic labels, not the same quantity as sensitivity, specificity, or execution completeness. & src/cellpack/positive\_control\_ladder.py; results/reports/cp\_q3\_positive\_null\_control\_ladder/method\_summary.csv \\
Route audit scalar & confidence & Deterministic route-assurance scalar emitted by each registered method/router rule and averaged descriptively in route summaries. & ContextGate emits 0.95 for strict or negative routes and 0.85 for abstentions; method-family comparators emit fixed scalars attached to their registered route rules. & Confidence is not a calibrated posterior probability and is not used as the FDR test or as an independent biological claim. & src/cellpack/contextgate.py; src/cellpack/method\_comparison.py; results/reports/cp\_q5\_contextgate\_decisions/contextgate\_decisions.csv \\
Aggregate operating point & FCR and sensitivity/specificity thresholds & Publication-facing policy thresholds: FCR <= 0.05, route recovery >= 0.95, sensitivity >= 0.65, and specificity >= 0.80. & Thresholds are checked at the aggregate ContextGate decision level and stress-tested against moderate and permissive operating points. & These are safety-first claim-release rules, not universal biological constants; exploratory users may relax them with explicit false-context exposure. & Supplementary Table S26; src/cellpack/contextgate.py; src/cellpack/robustness\_stress.py \\
Registration & registered artifact & A dataset, method, threshold, schema, command, or result table materialized in repository manifests and artifact registries before manuscript claim release. & Registry entries link artifact paths, generating commands, schema expectations, benchmark phases, and validation checks under version control. & Registered here means version-controlled benchmark registration; it is distinct from clinical-trial preregistration or an external timestamped DOI until release deposition. & data/manifests/contextgate\_benchmark\_manifest.yaml; data/manifests/artifact\_registry.yaml; manuscript/tables/manuscript\_table\_manifest.json \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
